question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an unbounded list of node.id=eq:X parameters to getNetworkNodes(), causing the HashSet<Long> nodeIds accumulation in NetworkServiceImpl to exhaust JVM heap memory, crashing the service and preventing the network from confirming new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a node.id parameter with operator GT and value Long.MAX_VALUE-1 so that getInclusiveValue() performs lowerBound++ causing a Long overflow, resulting in lowerBound wrapping to Long.MIN_VALUE, bypassing the lowerBound > upperBound guard and triggering an unbounded database scan that shuts down the service?
